initial_program:
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC-AGI transformation: copy the most frequent colour (maxColor)
%  into a 33 pattern. The pattern is built from the row and column
%  that contain the most occurrences of maxColor in the 33
%  subgrid (rows 02, cols 02).  The centre cell (1,1) is always
%  coloured with maxColor.  Additionally the two opposite corners are
%  coloured, and the centre of the opposite row/column is coloured.
%  The opposite corner (rowSel, oppCol) is coloured only when that
%  row has a strictly higher count of maxColour than its opposite row;
%  the opposite column corner (oppRow, colSel) is coloured only when
%  that column has a strictly higher count than its opposite column.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------

% ---- 1.  Determine the colour that occurs most often (nonzero)
count(C) = #count{ R : input(R, _, C) }.
maxCount = #max{ C : count(C) }.
maxColour = #min{ C : count(C) = maxCount, C != 0 }.

% ---- 2.  Count the maxColour inside the 33 subgrid (rows 02, cols 02)
%    The counts are stored in the variables rowCnt[r] and colCnt[c].
rowCnt(R) = #count{ Rr : input(Rr, _, maxColour), Rr = R, R < 3 }.
colCnt(C) = #count{ Cc : input(_, Cc, maxColour), Cc = C, C < 3 }.

% ---- 3.  Choose the row (rowSel) and column (colSel) that contain the
%       most occurrences of maxColour inside the subgrid.  Tiebreaks
%       favour the smaller index.
maxRow = #max{ R : rowCnt(R) }.
rowSel = #min{ R : rowCnt(R) = maxRow }.

maxCol = #max{ C : colCnt(C) }.
colSel = #min{ C : colCnt(C) = maxCol }.

oppRow = 2 - rowSel.
oppCol = 2 - colSel.

% ---- 4.  Build the output pattern
output(rowSel, colSel, maxColour).                     % corner (rowSel,colSel)

output(rowSel, C, maxColour) :- C < 3, C != colSel.   % whole row rowSel, without the column colSel

output(R, colSel, maxColour) :- R < 3, R != rowSel.   % whole column colSel, without the row rowSel

output(1, 1, maxColour) :- true.                     % centre cell

output(rowSel, oppCol, maxColour) :- count(rowSel) #> count(oppRow).   % optional (row, opposite column)

output(oppRow, colSel, maxColour) :- count(colSel) #> count(oppCol).   % optional (opposite row, column)

output(oppRow, oppCol, maxColour) :- true.           % opposite corner

% ---- 5.  Hide auxiliary predicates  only the final output is shown
#show output/3.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC-AGI transformation: copy the most frequent colour (maxColor)
%  into a 33 pattern. The pattern is built from the row and column
%  that contain the most occurrences of maxColor in the 33
%  subgrid (rows 02, cols 02).  The centre cell (1,1) is always
%  coloured with maxColor.  Additionally the two opposite corners are
%  coloured, and the centre of the opposite row/column is coloured.
%  The opposite corner (rowSel, oppCol) is coloured only when that
%  row has a strictly higher count of maxColour than its opposite row;
%  the opposite column corner (oppRow, colSel) is coloured only when
%  that column has a strictly higher count than its opposite column.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------

% ---- 1.  Determine the colour that occurs most often (nonzero)
count(C) = #count{ R : input(R, _, C) }.
maxCount = #max{ C : count(C) }.
maxColour = #min{ C : count(C) = maxCount }.

% ---- 2.  Count the maxColour inside the 33 subgrid (rows 02, cols 02)
%    The counts are stored in the variables rowCnt[r] and colCnt[c].
rowCnt(R) = #count{ Rr : input(Rr, _, maxColour), Rr = R }.
colCnt(C) = #count{ Cc : input(_, Cc, maxColour), Cc = C }.

% ---- 3.  Choose the row (rowSel) and column (colSel) that contain the
%       most occurrences of maxColour inside the subgrid.  Tiebreaks
%       favour the smaller index.
maxRow = #max{ R : rowCnt(R), R < 3 }.
rowSel = #min{ R : rowCnt(R) = maxRow }.

maxCol = #max{ C : colCnt(C), C < 3 }.
colSel = #min{ C : colCnt(C) = maxCol }.

oppRow = 2 - rowSel.
oppCol = 2 - colSel.

% ---- 4.  Build the output pattern
output(rowSel, colSel, maxColour).                     % corner (rowSel,colSel)

output(rowSel, C, maxColour) :- C < 3, C != colSel.   % whole row rowSel, without the column colSel

output(R, colSel, maxColour) :- R < 3, R != rowSel.   % whole column colSel, without the row rowSel

output(1, 1, maxColour) :- true.                     % centre cell

output(rowSel, oppCol, maxColour) :- count(rowSel) > count(oppRow).

output(oppRow, colSel, maxColour) :- count(colSel) > count(oppCol).

output(oppRow, oppCol, maxColour) :- true.           % opposite corner

% ---- 5.  Hide auxiliary predicates  only the final output is shown
#show output/3.

output(rowSel, colSel, maxColour).                     % corner (rowSel,colSel)

output(rowSel, C, maxColour) :- C < 3, C != colSel.   % whole row rowSel, without the column colSel

output(R, colSel, maxColour) :- R < 3, R != rowSel.   % whole column colSel, without the row rowSel

output(1, 1, maxColour) :- true.                     % centre cell

output(rowSel, oppCol, maxColour) :- count(rowSel) > count(oppRow).

output(oppRow, colSel, maxColour) :- count(colSel) > count(oppCol).

output(oppRow, oppCol, maxColour) :- true.           % opposite corner

% ---- 5.  Hide auxiliary predicates  only the final output is shown
#show output/3.
